findings.detail: Contoso Lead Scoring & Routing: Cloud flow points at different Teams channel IDs and lead-score thresholds in dev/test vs prod because values are hardcoded rather than sourced from environment variables.
SalesStageProgressBar: PCF control is a newer version (with probability tooltip) in dev/test but prod still runs the prior build; the control was updated outside the managed solution import.

svc-sentiment-scoring-fn: Test and Prod point the CSAT sync flow at region-specific Azure Function endpoints, but Dev still references a developer's personal Function App URL hardcoded in the flow rather than an environment variable, so the flow behaves differently across environments.

con_SlaTimerControl: The PCF SLA timer control is deployed at v1.2 in Dev/Test but Prod is pinned to v1.0; the newer warning-threshold color logic is not yet promoted, so timers render differently in Prod.

SLA Breach Risk Escalation (cloud flow): In Dev the Teams connector points at a personal test channel and the flow is turned on; in Test it is off; in Prod it targets the Dispatch Operations channel. The Teams channel ID is hardcoded rather than sourced from an environment variable, causing environment drift.

Copilot for Field Service: Enabled and configured against the mobile app in Prod, but the Copilot feature toggle is disabled in the Test environment, so pre-prod validation of summarization prompts is not possible.

con-InvoicePDF-LogicApp: The connection reference target differs across environments — DEV points at a sandbox SharePoint site and a test SMTP, while PROD points at the corporate document library; the flow was hand-edited in PROD rather than deployed via the solution, so the two are out of sync.

con_ExpenseReceiptExtraction: The AI Builder receipt model is trained and published in DEV/TEST but only a stub (unpublished) model exists in PROD, so con_receiptverified auto-population silently no-ops in production.

Contoso Send-Time Optimization API: The custom API and its Azure ML endpoint environment variable point at a sandbox model in dev/test but are unregistered in prod, so journey send-time steps silently fall back to default timing in production.

con_legalfootertemplate: Prod contains an extra APAC legal footer template row and an additional con_region option (APAC) that were hotfixed directly in production and never backported to dev, causing schema/data drift.

Match Rule Override (con_matchrule): Prod runs 3 additional fuzzy match rules (higher precision threshold) that were hotfixed directly in production and never back-ported to dev/test, so unification results differ across environments.

OneLake / ADLS Gen2 Ingestion data source (msdynci_datasource): Dev/test point at a sampled ADLS Gen2 container while prod ingests from Microsoft Fabric OneLake; the msdynci_sourcetype value therefore differs, causing schema-refresh diffs.

Dual-write table mapping (mserp_vendinvoiceheader): TEST maps only vendinvoice header fields while PROD includes the vendinvoice-to-con_apapprovalrequest write-back mapping, so approval write-back cannot be validated in TEST before release.

Contoso AP Copilot: The Copilot Studio agent is published and grounded on live mserp_vendinvoiceheader data in PROD but points at a stale knowledge source in DEV, producing different answers across environments.

Generate PO in F&O on Approval: Cloud flow points at the F&O UAT legal entity (USMF) connection reference in test but the production legal entity (CONT) in prod; the dev copy is turned off entirely, so PO generation cannot be validated in dev.
con_purchaserequisition: The con_generatedponumber column and its business rule exist in dev and test but were never deployed to prod, leaving the PO write-back silently no-op in production.

ChannelDataPublish-LogicApp: The Logic App HTTP endpoint URL is hardcoded in the 'Channel Data Publish Trigger' flow rather than stored in an environment variable, so dev/test point at the sandbox Commerce Scale Unit while prod points at the production CSU — the flow drifts per environment.
LoyaltyTierGauge: The LoyaltyTierGauge PCF control is at v1.2 in dev/test but only v1.0 in prod; the newer version reads con_tierstructure which does not yet render correctly in production.

New Hire Onboarding Orchestration: The onboarding flow points at the Test M365 tenant's Office 365 Users connection in test but the production tenant in prod; the dev copy is turned off, so an onboarding task template edit made in dev was never promoted.
OrgChartControl: PCF OrgChartControl is at v1.2.0 in prod but v1.4.0-preview in dev/test, so the position hierarchy renders differently and the newer collapse feature is missing in production.
Contoso Contact Center Copilot: Bound to a Copilot Studio dev bot in DEV/TEST but points to the production-published bot in PROD; con_botconfiguration record GUIDs differ across environments.
Sync Channel Budget from Finance: Nightly recurrence flow is enabled in PROD only; disabled in DEV/TEST because dual-write to Dynamics 365 Finance is not provisioned in the lower environments.

environmentvariabledefinition (con_CoEAdminTeamEmail / con_DlpPolicyName): Environment variable current values differ across environments - Dev points at a test distribution list and a permissive DLP policy, while Prod uses the real CoE DL and the enforced enterprise DLP policy. Only the definition is solution-aware; values drift per environment.

Con.Governance.DataSensitivityGuardPlugin (sdkmessageprocessingstep): The synchronous plug-in step is registered and active in Prod but disabled in Dev/Test to let makers freely edit sensitivity during development, meaning the sensitivity-downgrade guard is only enforced in Production.
ContosoContactCenter exists in Dev Integration/ORT but is absent from UAT/Production.
ContosoPlatformGovernance exists in Dev Integration/ORT/UAT but is absent from Production.
ContosoFieldService version differs across environments (1.3.0.0, 1.3.0.0, 1.3.0.0, 1.2.0.0).
ContosoProjectOperations version differs across environments (1.4.0.0, 1.4.0.0, 1.3.0.0, 1.3.0.0).
ContosoMarketing version differs across environments (1.5.0.0, 1.5.0.0, 1.5.0.0, 1.4.0.0).
ContosoCustomerInsights version differs across environments (1.6.0.0, 1.6.0.0, 1.5.0.0, 1.5.0.0).
ContosoFinance version differs across environments (1.7.0.0, 1.7.0.0, 1.7.0.0, 1.6.0.0).
